sections.paragraph:
βελτιόω (s. βελτίων; Plut., Mor. 85c; SIG 888, 5; StudPal XX, 86, 19; pap, e.g. PCairMasp 97 recto, 36; PLond III, 1044, 22 p. 255; Philo, Det. Pot. Ins. 56, Sacr. Abel. 42) to enhance in value or quality, improve, pass. become better of the soul (cp. Philo, Dec. 17) Dg 6:9.—DELG s.v. βέλτερος.

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

StudPal StudPal = CWessely, Studien zur Paläographie und Papyrusurkunden—List 4

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

PCairMasp PCairMasp = JMaspero, Papyrus grecs d’époque byzantine—List 4

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 174
